lede: A look at July 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Agustina Roth and Aaron Gillespie, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kyoto Animation arson attack
events.desc: A man sets fire to an anime studio in Fushimi-ku, Kyoto, Japan, killing 36 people and injuring dozens of others.

events.title: Islamic State
events.desc: The Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant requires Christians to either accept dhimmi status, emigrate from ISIL lands, or be killed.

events.title: 2012 Burgas bus bombing
events.desc: At least seven people are killed and 32 others are injured after a bomb explodes on an Israeli tour bus at Burgas Airport, Bulgaria.

events.title: San Ysidro McDonald's massacre
events.desc: McDonald's massacre in San Ysidro, California: James Oliver Huberty kills 21 people and injures 19 others before being shot dead by police.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: During the Beisfjord massacre in Norway, 15 Norwegian paramilitary guards help members of the SS to kill 288 political prisoners from Yugoslavia.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Second Battle of Fort Wagner: One of the first formal African American military units, the 54th Massachusetts Volunteer Infantry, supported by several white regiments, attempts an unsuccessful assault on Confederate-held Battery Wagner.

events.title: Pedro II of Brazil
events.desc: Coronation of Emperor Pedro II of Brazil.

events.title: 1806 Birgu polverista explosion
events.desc: A gunpowder magazine explosion in Birgu, Malta, kills around 200 people.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Johann Sebastian Bach leads the first performance of his cantata Erforsche mich, Gott, und erfahre mein Herz, BWV 136, in Leipzig on the eighth Sunday after Trinity.

events.title: Edward I
events.desc: King Edward I of England issues the Edict of Expulsion, banishing all Jews (numbering about 16,000) from England.

births.name: Roald Hoffmann
births.line: Polish chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Andrei Gromyko
births.line: Belarusian-Russian economist and politician, Soviet Minister of Foreign Affairs (died 1989)

deaths.text: Francisco Javier Arana, Guatemalan Army colonel and briefly Guatemalan head of state
François de Neufville, 2nd Duke of Villeroy, duc de Villeroy, French general

observances.text: Christian feast day: Camillus de Lellis (optional memorial, United States only)
Christian feast day: Eadburh (or Edburga) of Bicester
Christian feast day: Elizabeth Ferard (Church of England)
